metadata.name: Manage Repeating Jobs by Using Systemd Timer Units
metadata.description: Schedule and inspect recurring system tasks that are scheduled as systemd timer units
objectives: Update the systemd timer unit that gathers system activity data
prerequisites: Understanding of system timers and scheduling

tasks.name: Verify sysstat package is installed
tasks.description: The sysstat package must be installed to provide system activity data collection
tasks.failure_message: The sysstat package is not installed
hints: Install the sysstat package: sudo dnf install sysstat

tasks.name: Verify custom timer configuration file exists
tasks.description: The sysstat-collect.timer file must be copied to /etc/systemd/system for customization
tasks.success_message: The custom timer configuration file exists
tasks.failure_message: The custom timer configuration file is missing

tasks.name: Verify timer is configured for 2-minute interval
tasks.description: The OnCalendar directive must be set to collect data every 2 minutes
tasks.success_message: Timer is correctly configured for 2-minute intervals
tasks.failure_message: Timer is not configured for 2-minute intervals

tasks.name: Verify sysstat-collect.timer is enabled
tasks.description: The timer unit must be enabled to start at boot
hints: Enable the timer: sudo systemctl enable sysstat-collect.timer

tasks.name: Verify sysstat-collect.timer is active
tasks.description: The timer unit must be running to schedule data collection
hints: Start the timer: sudo systemctl start sysstat-collect.timer
Or enable and start: sudo systemctl enable --now sysstat-collect.timer
